title: Use of SB_GB_IO
body:
First of all, I apologise for this 'cos I suspect strongly the problem is somewhere between my keyboard and my chair, however, I am having huge difficulties instantiating a SB_GB_IO.  The original clock comes from a pin on the device and I want to distribute it internally, so I thought this would be sufficient;

```
SB_GB_IO BtraceClk0
(
  .PACKAGE_PIN(traceClk),
  .GLOBAL_BUFFER_OUTPUT(BtraceClk)
);
```

however, when I try to synthesise this, I get;

```
read_blif tracIF.blif...
prune...
arachne-pnr: src/netlist.cc:657: void Model::check(const Design*) const: Assertion `!p2->is_bidir()' failed.
./create: line 2:  4153 Aborted                 (core dumped) arachne-pnr -d 8k -P ct256 -p toplevel.pcf tracIF.blif -o traceIF.txt
```

I'm afraid I'm a bit stuck. Sorry, I know this isn't a tech support forum, but the assertion barf means I don't really know how to move forward with this.

DAVE
code_blocks:
read_blif tracIF.blif...
prune...
arachne-pnr: src/netlist.cc:657: void Model::check(const Design*) const: Assertion `!p2->is_bidir()' failed.
./create: line 2:  4153 Aborted                 (core dumped) arachne-pnr -d 8k -P ct256 -p toplevel.pcf tracIF.blif -o traceIF.txt
